mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance on various hardware accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments where low latency and high throughput are critical. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for specific infrastructure requirements.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to your PATH.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.31

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified zone.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command fetches the cluster credentials and updates your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, which simplifies deployment and management.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> This command adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> This command installs llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> This command applies the autoscaling configuration.
> This command applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This command uninstalls the llm-d release.
> This command deletes the GKE cluster and all orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> This command backs up the current deployment configuration.
> This command upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running and ready after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or the repository is unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and repository URL in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' for details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your GKE cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service may not be properly exposed or the application is not running.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the application pods are running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and cloud CLI installed, and that your cluster meets the resource requirements.
